# Methods

## Pipeline Architecture

meta-pipe is organized into 10 sequential stages, each implemented as a self-contained skill module with defined inputs, outputs, and quality thresholds (@tbl-stages).
The pipeline follows a modular architecture in which each stage can be executed independently while maintaining data flow through standardized file formats (CSV, BibTeX, and Quarto markdown).

<!-- TODO: Insert Table 1 reference -->

Human decision points are embedded at five critical junctures: (1) PICO definition and eligibility criteria; (2) screening disagreement resolution; (3) analysis type selection (pairwise vs network meta-analysis); (4) GRADE quality assessment; and (5) interpretation and clinical implications.

## Technology Stack

The pipeline integrates four complementary technologies:

1. **LLM orchestration**: Claude (Anthropic) serves as the AI backbone, operating through a skill-based agent architecture. Each pipeline stage has a dedicated skill module containing workflow guidance, command references, and validation criteria.

2. **Python automation**: Twenty-four Python scripts (~3,600 lines of code) handle data processing, format conversion, LLM-assisted screening and extraction, validation, and quality assurance. Package management uses uv with virtual environments.

3. **R statistical engine**: The meta and metafor packages perform meta-analysis computations, including random-effects models (REML estimation with Hartung-Knapp adjustment), heterogeneity assessment, publication bias diagnostics, and subgroup analyses. Package versions are locked via renv for reproducibility.

4. **Quarto rendering**: Manuscript assembly uses Quarto with Typst backend, generating HTML, DOCX, and PDF outputs with integrated figures, tables, and citations.

## Search and Screening (Stages 01-03)

Literature searches are conducted in a minimum of two databases (PubMed and Scopus, per PRISMA requirements), with optional extension to Embase and the Cochrane Library.
Search strategies are developed using PICO-derived terms combined with Boolean operators and Medical Subject Headings.

Title and abstract screening employs LLM-assisted classification with a dual-review design.
Inter-rater agreement is quantified using Cohen's kappa, with a minimum threshold of 0.60 for acceptable agreement.
Disagreements are resolved through human adjudication.

## Data Extraction and Quality Assessment (Stages 04-05)

Full-text retrieval uses Unpaywall API integration supplemented by manual acquisition.
Data extraction employs LLM-assisted parsing of PDF documents with a structured extraction template defining all required fields.

Extracted data undergo automated validation including range checks, consistency verification, and confidence scoring.
Low-confidence extractions are flagged for manual review.
Risk of bias is assessed using the Cochrane RoB 2 tool for randomized trials or ROBINS-I for observational studies.

## Statistical Analysis (Stage 06)

Pairwise meta-analyses use random-effects models with restricted maximum likelihood estimation and the Hartung-Knapp adjustment for confidence intervals.
Heterogeneity is quantified using the I^2^ statistic, Cochran's Q test, and prediction intervals.

Publication bias is assessed using funnel plots, Egger's regression test, and trim-and-fill analysis.
Sensitivity analyses include leave-one-out analysis, influence diagnostics, and subgroup analyses by prespecified variables.

For comparisons involving three or more interventions, network meta-analysis is performed using Bayesian methods (gemtc package) with frequentist sensitivity analysis (netmeta package).

## Manuscript Generation (Stage 07)

Manuscript assembly uses a template-based approach with Quarto includes for each IMRaD section.
The pipeline generates structured content from analysis outputs, including result paragraphs with effect estimates, confidence intervals, and p-values.

Automated scripts handle PRISMA flow diagram generation, citation management, figure and table integration, and journal-specific formatting.
Word counts and section structure are validated against target journal requirements.

## Quality Assurance (Stages 08-09)

Publication readiness is quantified using an objective 0-100% score comprising eight components: PRISMA compliance, citation completeness, figure quality (>=300 DPI), statistical reporting standards, GRADE assessment, overclaim detection, word count compliance, and supplementary material completeness.

The overclaim detection system identifies 12 common patterns of unsupported claims, including causal language for observational data, generalization beyond study populations, and recommendations without sufficient evidence level.

## Demonstration Study

To demonstrate the pipeline, we conducted a meta-analysis of immune checkpoint inhibitors added to neoadjuvant chemotherapy for triple-negative breast cancer.
The PICO framework was defined as follows: (P) patients with early-stage TNBC; (I) immune checkpoint inhibitor plus neoadjuvant chemotherapy; (C) neoadjuvant chemotherapy alone; (O) pathologic complete response (primary), event-free survival, overall survival, and serious adverse events.

Eligibility was restricted to phase II-III randomized controlled trials published through 2025.
